title: papaver atlanticum
intro: Papaver atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic poppy or meadow poppy, is a flowering plant native to Europe and North Africa. It contains alkaloids, including thebaine and codeine, which have been used for medicinal purposes. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including pain, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The alkaloids can be extracted and used to prepare various herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, for pain relief, wound healing, and other medicinal purposes.
